description_long: This stone is an irregularly shaped, medium-sized rock with a predominantly light gray to off-white coloration, interspersed with darker gray and black streaks and patches. The surface features a combination of smooth and rough textures, with visible natural veining and banding that suggest a metamorphic or sedimentary origin. Some faces of the stone are relatively flat, while others are jagged and uneven, giving it a rugged, natural appearance. The stone appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or artificial modifications. Its coloration and patterning make it visually striking, especially in contrast to uniform urban surfaces. The overall form and size suggest it could serve as a natural obstacle or decorative element in an urban landscape.
description: A rugged, irregular stone with light gray and dark veining, featuring both smooth and rough surfaces. Its natural banding and solid form make it visually distinct.
description_view_0: This view highlights the stone's prominent dark gray and black veining on a light gray base, with a jagged, angular profile.
description_view_1: This side shows a flatter, lighter surface with minimal veining and a more uniform off-white coloration.
description_view_2: Here, the stone's banded pattern is visible, with swirling gray lines and a rough, uneven texture.
description_view_3: This angle reveals a mix of smooth and rough areas, with subtle veining and a rounded, weathered appearance.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or partially embedded in the ground
attribute_pattern: Natural veining and banding with irregular, swirling gray and black lines across the surface
attribute_weathering: Slightly weathered with a mix of smooth and rough areas, indicating moderate exposure to the elements
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved surfaces but can blend with other natural stones or landscaping elements, often partially surrounded by soil or grass
